title: Poems From "A Shropshire Lad" - VI
content:
When the lad for longing sighs,
    Mute and dull of cheer and pale,
    If at death's own door he lies,
    Maiden, you can heal his ail.

    Lovers' ills are all to buy:
    The wan look, the hollow tone,
    The hung head, the sunken eye,
    You can have them for your own.

    Buy them, buy them: eve and morn
    Lovers' ills are all to sell.
    Then you can lie down forlorn;
    But the lover will be well.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Alfred Edward Housman, titled "Poems From "A Shropshire Lad" - VI", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
